title: dianthus orientalis subsp. nassireddinii
intro: Dianthus orientalis subsp. nassireddinii, also known as the Turkish pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and wounds, due to its health benefits. The herbal preparations of Dianthus orientalis subsp. nassireddinii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
